school_name: Alice Springs School Of The Air

overview: Casuarina Senior College Pre-Academy: Health and STEM offers high-achieving Year 10 students a unique and dedicated pathway through their senior studies, leading to tertiary education. The pathway includes specialised courses that provide students with opportunities for enrichment and acceleration, beginning in Year 10 with Stage 1 subjects and progressing to Stage 2 advanced study.
description: The Pre-Academy program is designed for high-achieving Year 10 students and offers early access to advanced study in health, science, and technology. In Year 10, students can study Stage 1 subjects and, in Semester 2, continue with Stage 1 subjects while also having the option to undertake a 10-credit Stage 2 subject. As part of the Stage 1 Pre-Academy Exploring Identities and Futures (EIF) subject, students complete a Work Experience placement. Students progress through the optional Stage 2 Pre-Academy Activating Identities and Futures (AIF) course, which may be undertaken in Semester 2 of Year 10. Through the Stage 2 Pre-Academy AIF subject, students can explore a passion project in Health or STEM. Entry is competitive and selection is based on ACER General Ability Test results combined with the application form and previous academic reports and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-achieving Year 10 students; Must be entering Year 10; Written application required; ACER General Ability Test required; Previous school reports required; NAPLAN results considered
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for ACER General Ability Test. Students from both pathways are encouraged to participate in relevant extra-curricular activities. All Centre for Excellence students are provided with opportunities to engage with external organisations including Charles Darwin University, Flinders University, P-TECH, Menzies School of Health Research, Power and Water Corporation, Territory Generation and Advanced Manufacturing Alliance. Students from both pathways are given the opportunity to be part of a trip to Perth at the end of their second year where they experience university life.

program_name: Centre for Excellence in Medicine and Health Sciences
overview: Established in 2011, the Centre for Excellence in Medicine and Health Sciences is a selective program for high-achieving students interested in health-related careers. The Centre provides opportunities for students to engage with leading health organisations and universities, preparing them for health science pathways.
description: The Centre for Excellence in Medicine and Health Sciences is a specialised program established in 2011 to enhance opportunities for students interested in health-related careers. The Centre values formal and informal partnerships with Charles Darwin University, Flinders Medical Centre, Menzies School of Health Research, NT Department of Health, Royal Darwin Hospital and Medicare Local. Students who gain a place undertake all of their NTCET studies at the school. Entry into the program is by selection as places are limited. Selection is based on completion of one or more selection criteria including assessment of academic ability, previous school reports, and NAPLAN results. Students must sit a test, with the results combined with the application form and previous reports and NAPLAN results used to determine eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating high academic performance; Selection-based entry; Must be capable of undertaking accelerated learning; NAPLAN results reviewed; Academic reports from previous school required
special_conditions: Entry is competitive and places are limited. Students are encouraged to participate in relevant extra-curricular activities and are provided with opportunities to engage with external organisations including Charles Darwin University, Flinders University, P-TECH, Menzies School of Health Research, Power and Water Corporation, Territory Generation and Advanced Manufacturing Alliance. Students given opportunity to undertake a trip to Perth at the end of their second year to experience university life and explore courses at Curtain University, Edith Cowan University, University of Western Australia and Murdoch University.

program_name: Centre for Excellence in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM)
overview: Expanded in 2017 from the original Centre for Excellence, this program offers high-achieving students dedicated pathways in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. The program includes access to state-of-the-art STEM facilities and engagement with industry partners.
description: The Centre for Excellence in STEM was established to support growing worldwide demand for skills in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. The College offers access to cutting-edge technology learning through a state-of-the-art STEM centre featuring flexible learning spaces, including a fab lab with digital printer and laser cutting machine, computer rooms, makerspace and workshop facilities. Students in the STEM centre are learning to become critical, creative thinkers with problem-solving skills that prepare them for increasingly digital futures. The program provides contemporary, cross-disciplinary approaches to teaching and learning in STEM-related fields. Entry is selective with places limited, and selection is based on demonstrated academic ability and potential in science and technology subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-achieving students with demonstrated ability in STEM subjects; Selection-based entry with limited places; Academic performance in science and technology subjects; May require assessment of STEM capability
special_conditions: Flexible learning spaces designed to maximise opportunities for various activities and student groups. College continues to grow its Pathways in Technology program with strengthened connections and development with industry partners. The different areas - computer technology, fab lab, makerspace and light industrial spaces - allow wide range of flexibility for use in all curriculum areas.

overview: Casuarina Senior College offers CDU UniStart, allowing students to get a head start on university by studying real university units while completing their NTCET. This program provides pathway opportunities for students aiming for higher education.
description: CDU UniStart provides eligible students with the opportunity to study real university units from Charles Darwin University while completing their Northern Territory Certificate of Education and Training (NTCET) at Casuarina Senior College. This early entry program allows students to gain university credits and experience before completing their secondary education, providing a head start towards their tertiary studies. Students undertake a combination of senior secondary and university-level coursework, with university credits earned being recognised towards future degree programs at Charles Darwin University or other institutions. The program is designed for students with strong academic performance and commitment to university-pathway subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to senior secondary students enrolled at Casuarina Senior College; Must demonstrate capacity to undertake university-level study while completing NTCET; Academic performance in NTCET subjects required; Interest in university pathway studies
special_conditions: Students study real university units as part of CDU partnership with Casuarina Senior College. University credits earned are recognised towards degree programs. Program available to students completing their NTCET at the College.

overview: Casuarina Senior College became the host school for the SEDA Program in 2013. SEDA (Sports Education Development Australia) is a community-based support program designed for young people who face challenges or barriers to engagement in education.
description: The SEDA Program is hosted at Casuarina Senior College to support young people who face challenges or barriers to engagement in education. The College values its partnership with SEDA which enables students to pursue their chosen goals through structured support, mentoring, and community engagement. SEDA operates as a sports development and education program that provides comprehensive support to help students remain engaged with school and develop life skills, leadership and personal resilience. The program combines education with structured activities and mentoring to support student wellbeing and engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students facing challenges or barriers to engagement in education; Selection based on identified need for support; Community referral pathway available; Students from Years 10-12 eligible; No specific academic performance threshold required
special_conditions: Program is community-based and designed for students facing barriers to engagement. Selection is not dependent on technical skills. College values formal partnership with SEDA enabling students to pursue chosen goals. Program includes mentoring, community engagement and structured support.

overview: Casuarina Senior College offers an Elite Sports Program, also known as the High Performance Sports Program, for student athletes. Launched in 2018, this program supports elite and high-performing athletes in pursuing excellence in both sport and academics.
description: The Elite Sports Program, launched in 2018 as the High Performance Sports Program, provides specialised support for student athletes competing at elite levels. The program is designed to help elite and high-performing athletes manage their training and competition commitments while successfully completing their senior secondary studies. The College employs elite sports program staff and performance psychologists to support student athletes. The program recognises that elite athletes require flexible scheduling, specialised coaching support, and integrated academic planning to achieve their sporting and educational goals. Students in the program maintain their academic progression while accessing tailored support for their sporting pursuits.
eligibility_criteria: Open to elite or high-performing student athletes; Demonstrated achievement or potential in competitive sport; Ability to manage sport commitments alongside NTCET studies; Selection-based on athletic performance and commitment
special_conditions: Program includes access to elite sports program staff and performance psychology support. College provides flexible timetabling to accommodate training and competition commitments. Program launched in 2018 as High Performance Sports Program. Students must balance sporting commitments with academic performance.

program_name: ACE (Achievement, Commitment, Excellence) High Achievers' Program
overview: Casuarina Senior College provides the ACE (Achievement, Commitment, Excellence) High Achievers' Program to recognise and support students demonstrating excellence across multiple domains of school life including academics, co-curricular participation and leadership.
description: The ACE High Achievers' Program recognises and supports students who demonstrate achievement, commitment and excellence across academic, co-curricular and leadership domains. The program is designed for well-rounded students who excel not only in their academic studies but also participate actively in school co-curricular activities and demonstrate leadership potential. Students in the program are supported to achieve their personal best across all areas of school engagement. The program provides recognition and support for students demonstrating sustained commitment to excellence in multiple areas of school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating high academic achievement; Evidence of excellence in co-curricular activities required; Leadership potential or demonstrated leadership; Sustained commitment to multiple areas of school engagement
special_conditions: Program recognises students who achieve across multiple domains. Selection based on demonstrated excellence in academics, co-curricular participation and leadership. Students are encouraged to participate in various extracurricular programs.

Good Shepherd Lutheran College - St Andrews Campus

overview: OLSH College offers the Bennett House Scholarship, named after one of the college's four house patrons. The scholarship recognises and supports students demonstrating leadership qualities and commitment to the college community.
description: The Bennett House Scholarship is awarded to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8 at OLSH College who demonstrate leadership potential. The scholarship is named after Brother Bennett, a former brother of the college. Students are not required to sit a test in order to apply for this scholarship. All applications are assessed on merit. A 90% attendance requirement applies each year - if attendance falls below 90%, the scholarship will no longer be applicable. The scholarship recognises students who embody the values of their house patron.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Not available to international students; 90% attendance required annually; Application-based selection only
special_conditions: Students must maintain 90% attendance each year or the scholarship will no longer apply. No test required - application based only.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College - Bath Street Campus
overview: OLSH College offers the Gillen House Scholarship, named after Mount Gillen, a significant mountain in the Alice Springs region. The scholarship recognises academic achievement and commitment to excellence.
description: The Gillen House Scholarship is awarded to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8 at OLSH College. The scholarship is named after Mount Gillen, a significant landmark in the Alice Springs region. Students are not required to sit a test in order to apply for this scholarship. All applications are assessed on merit. A 90% attendance requirement applies each year - if attendance falls below 90%, the scholarship will no longer be applicable. The scholarship provides support to students demonstrating academic excellence and commitment to their studies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Not available to international students; 90% attendance required annually; Application-based selection only
special_conditions: Students must maintain 90% attendance each year or the scholarship will no longer apply. No test required - application based only.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College - Bath Street Campus
overview: OLSH College offers the Achoa House Scholarship, named after a word meaning 'friendship' in Arrernte, the native Indigenous language of the Alice Springs region. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous and culturally diverse students.
description: The Achoa House Scholarship is awarded to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8 at OLSH College. The scholarship is named after 'Achoa', which means 'friendship' in Arrernte, the native Indigenous language of the Alice Springs region. Students are not required to sit a test in order to apply for this scholarship. All applications are assessed on merit. A 90% attendance requirement applies each year - if attendance falls below 90%, the scholarship will no longer be applicable. The scholarship reflects the college's commitment to supporting Indigenous students and fostering cultural diversity within the community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Not available to international students; 90% attendance required annually; Application-based selection only
special_conditions: Students must maintain 90% attendance each year or the scholarship will no longer apply. No test required - application based only.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College - Bath Street Campus
overview: OLSH College offers the Standley House Scholarship, named after Ida Standley, one of the first teachers in Alice Springs. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate community service and leadership commitment.
description: The Standley House Scholarship is awarded to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8 at OLSH College. The scholarship is named after Ida Standley, one of the first teachers in Alice Springs, and recognises her legacy in education. Students are not required to sit a test in order to apply for this scholarship. Applications are assessed on merit, with consideration given to demonstrated leadership and community involvement. A 90% attendance requirement applies each year - if attendance falls below 90%, the scholarship will no longer be applicable. The scholarship supports students who embody values of service and community contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Awarded to members of any Catholic Parish Community demonstrating leadership in parish and sacramental life; Not available to international students; 90% attendance required annually; Application-based selection only
special_conditions: Awarded to members of any Catholic Parish Community for demonstrating leadership in Parish and Sacramental life. Students must maintain 90% attendance each year or the scholarship will no longer apply. No test required - application based only.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College - Bath Street Campus
overview: OLSH College offers St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarships to support students undertaking or intending to undertake music tuition. The scholarship recognises musical achievement and commitment to instrumental music performance.
description: The St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarship provides tuition fee remission for instrumental or vocal lessons (30 minute lessons) based on a maximum amount each term, plus instrumental hire if needed for one year. Tuition is available in woodwind, brass, guitar, piano and voice. The scholarship is available to both existing and new students at OLSH College from any year level. Students are not required to sit a test or perform an audition in order to apply for this scholarship. Applications are assessed based on the student's commitment to music and engagement in relevant school ensembles. Recipients must attend their weekly lesson, participate in relevant school ensembles and any associated performances. Other incidentals and additional lessons are not covered by the scholarship. A number of St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarships may be awarded each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently undertaking or intending to undertake music tuition; Available to existing and new students starting at OLSH College from any year level; Students studying a particular instrument; Not required to sit test or perform audition; Must participate in relevant school ensembles and performances
special_conditions: Recipients must attend their weekly lesson and participate in relevant school ensembles and any associated performances. Scholarships awarded with regard to the number of students already learning the particular instrument. Application requires minimum 200-word statement outlining commitment to music and evidence of achievement. Other incidentals and additional lessons beyond the scope of tuition are not covered.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College - Bath Street Campus
overview: OLSH College provides bursaries and financial assistance packages to support families seeking quality Catholic education. These programs are designed to ensure affordability for families across all socioeconomic circumstances.
description: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College offers a range of bursaries and financial assistance packages to ensure that fees are not a barrier to families seeking quality Catholic education. OLSH has a three-band fee structure approach to support diverse family circumstances. The college works with families on an individual basis to discuss fee structures that may be more suitable to their circumstances. Families are welcome to contact the Business Manager, Fiona Miller, to discuss available support options. Financial assistance is available for students from all age groups and year levels at the college. The college is committed to making Catholic education accessible and affordable for the Alice Springs community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Not available to international students; Current OLSH students eligible to apply; Families demonstrating financial need eligible for consideration
special_conditions: Contact Business Manager Fiona Miller at accounts@olsh.catholic.edu.au or phone 8269 8800 to discuss fee structures and financial assistance options. College operates a three-band fee approach.

school_name: Nyangatjatjara College - Kaltukatjara Campus (Docker River) Primary Campus
Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic College
overview: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic College offers scholarship and bursary programs to support families seeking Catholic education. The program provides financial assistance to current students progressing through the college.
description: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Catholic College invites applications for the 2026 OLSH Scholarship and Bursary Program available to eligible current students. The program provides financial assistance through various scholarships and bursaries, though specific program names, financial values, and selection criteria are not publicly detailed on the college website. Applicants are required to be current OLSH students enrolling in Years 5 through Year 12 for 2026. Scholarships and bursaries are not available to international students. Families interested in specific program details, financial amounts, and application procedures should contact the college directly.
eligibility_criteria: Current OLSH students; Entering Year 5 through to Year 12 in 2026; Not available to international students
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship and bursary program is available for current OLSH students only. International students are not eligible to apply.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Thamarrurr Catholic College

overview: Rosebery Secondary School (formerly Palmerston College Rosebery Campus) offers the AIM Program for Achieving, Inspired and Motivated learners. This selective entry program caters to a select group of high-achieving, motivated students who are inspired to learn in the Palmerston region. The program extends academic learning beyond the classroom and develops higher-order thinking skills.
description: The AIM Program is designed to challenge and extend the most academically able students while developing higher-order thinking skills and allowing deeper study into areas of interest. Students also develop academic, social and personal resilience through this exclusive program. Selection is based on strong academic performance across learning areas, evidence of academic engagement and growth, and demonstration of motivation to learn. The program provides curriculum pathways from secondary middle years through to senior secondary courses at Rosebery Secondary School, building on existing programs in primary schools. Families must submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) form along with required documentation to the school. The school expects to contact applicants early in Term 3 for applicants seeking entry in 2026 or 2027. No specific financial benefit value is stated on the program page, indicating this is a selective academic program rather than a financial assistance program.
eligibility_criteria: Strong academic performance across learning areas in primary school; Evidence of academic engagement and growth; Demonstration of motivation to learn; Entry in Year 7 or subsequent years (for entry in 2026 or 2027); Students in the Palmerston region; Expression of Interest form completion required
special_conditions: This is a select entry program. Applicants must complete and return the EOI form along with required documents to the front office at Palmerston College or email to palmerston.college@education.nt.gov.au. Contact from the school is expected early Term 3 for those applying for entry in 2026 or 2027. The program is designed to extend and challenge learning beyond the classroom while developing resilience.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Terriory Rugby League Stadium campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers a Scholarship Program that celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The program recognises students who demonstrate excellence and make positive contributions to the College community.
description: SEDA College NT is committed to supporting students and families through a range of opportunities that recognise excellence and provide financial assistance. The Scholarship Program celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The College also partners with local businesses and organisations to offer Sponsored Scholarships, creating valuable connections between students and industry. However, specific details regarding the exact financial value, duration, selection process, and renewal conditions are not publicly detailed on the College website. Interested applicants should contact the College directly for comprehensive program information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12 students; Must demonstrate achievement and contribution across key areas of College life; Specific academic and conduct thresholds not publicly specified
special_conditions: Specific renewal conditions and program details are not publicly available on the College website. Contact the College Admissions Team for detailed information.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Terriory Rugby League Stadium campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers Fee Assistance in the form of bursaries to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. This program provides support to eligible families to help them access education at the College.
description: The College may offer fee assistance (bursaries) to families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Due to limited funds being available, not all applicants who apply for fee assistance will be successful. College Fee Account Holders with a genuine need who would like to be considered for fee assistance should complete a Fee Assistance Application Form, which can be requested by emailing finance@sedacollege.nt.edu.au. College Fee Account Holders may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application. The financial assistance available varies depending on individual circumstances and fund availability.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current College Fee Account Holder; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Must complete a Fee Assistance Application Form; May be required to attend an interview
special_conditions: Limited funds available; not all applicants will be successful. Applications assessed on individual basis. Interview may be required to discuss application details.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Terriory Rugby League Stadium campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers Indigenous Leaders Scholarships in partnership with local businesses and organisations. These scholarships support Indigenous students demonstrating leadership potential and commitment to their educational journey.
description: In 2025, five SEDA College NT students received Indigenous Leaders Scholarships from partner organisations including DDR Australia, Vicinity Centres, Intract Australia, McMahon Services and MSS Security. These scholarships provide financial contributions towards tuition and educational costs for eligible Indigenous students. The program is part of the College's partnership with local businesses and organisations that are committed to supporting Indigenous student success. Financial value, specific eligibility criteria, and detailed application processes are determined by individual scholarship partners. Interested Indigenous students should contact the College Admissions Team for information about available sponsored scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Indigenous Australian or Torres Strait Islander; Open to Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12 students; Demonstrated commitment to educational journey; Leadership potential
special_conditions: Scholarships provided through partnerships with external organisations. Financial value and specific terms vary by partner organisation. Contact College for individual partner requirements.

overview: SEDA College NT offers a Scholarship Program that celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The program recognises students who demonstrate excellence and make positive contributions to the college community.
description: SEDA College NT maintains a Scholarship Program that celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The College partners with local businesses and organisations to offer Sponsored Scholarships, creating valuable connections between students and industry. However, the college does not publish detailed public information about specific scholarship categories, financial values, selection criteria, or application processes on their website. Interested families are advised to contact the admissions team directly at admissions@sedacollege.nt.edu.au or 1300 337 332 for comprehensive details about available scholarships. The College is a registered not-for-profit organisation committed to supporting students and families through opportunities that recognise excellence and provide financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12 students; Specific eligibility criteria not publicly detailed; Applicants should contact admissions team for requirements
special_conditions: Specific scholarship conditions, renewal terms, and duration not publicly detailed on the college website. Families should contact the college directly for complete information about scholarship terms and conditions.

overview: SEDA College NT offers Fee Assistance (Bursaries) to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The program aims to remove financial barriers for students who would otherwise be unable to attend the college.
description: SEDA College NT may offer fee assistance (bursaries) to families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Due to limited funds being available, not all applications are successful. College Fee Account Holders with a genuine need who would like to be considered for fee assistance should complete a Fee Assistance Application Form, which can be requested by emailing finance@sedacollege.nt.edu.au. Applicants may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their financial circumstances and application. The college assesses applications on a case-by-case basis and determines the amount of assistance based on individual need and available funding.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current or enrolled students at SEDA College NT; Family experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Must be a College Fee Account Holder; Application based on genuine financial need
special_conditions: Due to limited funds being available, not all debtor account holders who apply for fee assistance will be successful. College Fee Account Holders may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application. Applications are assessed on individual circumstances.

overview: SEDA College NT coordinates Indigenous Leaders Scholarships in partnership with external sponsors including DDR Australia, Vicinity Centres, Intract Australia, McMahon Services and MSS Security. These scholarships support Indigenous students who are committed to reaching their full potential.
description: In 2025, five SEDA College NT students received an Indigenous Leaders Scholarship from DDR Australia, Vicinity Centres, Intract Australia, McMahon Services and MSS Security. These sponsored scholarships provide financial contributions towards supporting Indigenous students' education and development. The scholarships are designed to empower young Indigenous people who are passionate about their chosen field of study, dedicated to reaching their full potential and committed to using the unique opportunities that SEDA College NT provides. Recipients are supported through their educational journey with both financial assistance and mentorship opportunities. The College works with corporate partners to create these valuable scholarships as part of its commitment to supporting Indigenous students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous/Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12 enrolment; Students seeking to reach their full potential; Demonstrated commitment to education; Sponsorship partnerships vary annually
special_conditions: Scholarship availability and number of awards vary year to year based on corporate sponsorship partnerships. Recipients are selected based on individual circumstances and commitment to their education. Recipients are expected to engage with mentorship and support programs offered by the College.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Waratah Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers the Rob Flower Scholarship to students completing their Senior Secondary Education at the College. The scholarship supports education, personal development and career opportunities for students who demonstrate commitment to SEDA's core values and make positive contributions to their community.
description: The Rob Flower Scholarship is awarded to successful applicants completing their Senior Secondary Education at SEDA College NT and will support their education, personal development and career opportunities. Assessment is not based on academic merit but considers the skills, interest and attributes of the applicant. A SEDA scholarship may be awarded to a student who is able to demonstrate that they will benefit from, and contribute to, the range of opportunities available through the SEDA program. Selection criteria include Community (commitment to community and contribution to local services in sport, recreation, arts or other areas), Effort (commitment to an educational or personal pursuit), Leadership (demonstration of leadership skills and willingness to support others), Equality (commitment to treating everyone as equal and maintaining respectful relationships), and Loyalty (contribution and support in friendship circles and the community). SEDA reserves discretion to determine the number of scholarships to be awarded and the value of each scholarship annually.
eligibility_criteria: Completing Senior Secondary Education at SEDA College NT; Demonstrated skills, interests and attributes aligned with SEDA program; Ability to benefit from and contribute to SEDA opportunities; Willingness to attend interview if requested
special_conditions: SEDA may in its discretion determine the number of scholarships to be awarded and the value of the scholarship. The total number of scholarships awarded will be determined by the SEDA Board annually or at such other times as the Board determines. Students must attend interview if requested by the College.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Waratah Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers the Indigenous Leaders Scholarship to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The scholarship provides financial support and recognises students' commitment to achieving their full potential and contributing to their community.
description: The Indigenous Leaders Scholarship is offered to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at SEDA College NT. The scholarship offers a comprehensive package of support including a scholarship covering Year 12 tuition fees (not including yearly deposit) and additional funding support for students' career and wellbeing aspirations. These scholarships are provided by corporate partners and external donors who are committed to supporting Indigenous young people in their educational journey. The number of scholarships provided each year varies depending on donors and available funding. Scholarships are monitored and reviewed at the end of each term; students who have not met their expectations and commitments will have their scholarships terminated.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander descent; Year 12 students at SEDA College NT; Demonstrated commitment to meeting expectations and scholarships conditions; Enrolled as full-time student at the College
special_conditions: Scholarships are monitored and reviewed at the end of each term. Students who have not met their expectations and commitments will have their scholarships terminated. The number of scholarships provided each year will vary depending on donors. Additional funding support is provided for students' career and wellbeing aspirations.

school_name: SEDA College NT - Waratah Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers Fee Assistance (Bursaries) to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The College is committed to ensuring that affordability is not a barrier to accessing quality secondary education at SEDA.
description: The College may offer fee assistance (bursaries) to families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Bursary support is provided on a needs-tested basis to eligible families enrolled at SEDA College NT. Due to limited funds being available, not all applicants who apply for fee assistance will be successful. Fee Account Holders with a genuine need who would like to be considered for fee assistance should complete a Fee Assistance Application Form, which can be requested by emailing the Finance team. Applicants may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application and circumstances. The College assesses applications on an individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Current student enrolled at SEDA College NT; Family experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Genuine financial hardship demonstrated; Completion of Fee Assistance Application Form required
special_conditions: Due to limited funds being available, not all debtor account holders who apply for fee assistance will be successful. Applications are assessed on an individual basis. Applicants may be asked to attend an interview with Finance team to discuss their application.

school_name: SEDA College NT - St Mary's Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers a Scholarship Program that celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The program recognises students who demonstrate excellence across academic and co-curricular activities.
description: SEDA College NT operates a Scholarship Program that recognises and rewards achievement and contribution across key areas of College life. The program celebrates students who demonstrate excellence in their studies and involvement in the College community. Specific financial benefits, selection processes, and application details are not publicly detailed on the website. Interested students are directed to contact the College for more information about scholarship opportunities. The exact value and conditions vary depending on individual circumstances and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled at SEDA College NT; Contact the College directly for specific scholarship eligibility requirements
special_conditions: Further information available by contacting the College directly.

school_name: SEDA College NT - St Mary's Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers Fee Assistance in the form of bursaries to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The program is designed to help remove financial barriers for students who would otherwise struggle to meet school fees.
description: The College may offer fee assistance (bursaries) to families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Due to limited funds being available, not all applicants who apply for fee assistance will be successful. The exact financial benefit is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual circumstances and family needs. Families seeking assistance must complete a Fee Assistance Application Form and may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application. The College reserves the right to determine the level of assistance based on demonstrated financial hardship and available funding.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a College Fee Account Holder; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Must complete a Fee Assistance Application Form; Families may be interviewed regarding their application
special_conditions: Due to limited funds being available, not all debtor account holders who apply for fee assistance will be successful. College Fee Account Holders with a genuine need may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application.

school_name: SEDA College NT - St Mary's Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT partners with local businesses and organisations to offer Sponsored Scholarships. These scholarships create valuable connections between students and industry partners while providing financial support.
description: The College partners with local businesses and organisations to offer Sponsored Scholarships, creating valuable connections between students and industry. These scholarships are funded by external sponsors who donate to support students' educational journeys. The financial benefits, eligibility criteria, and conditions vary depending on the individual sponsor and their specific objectives. Past sponsors have included companies such as DDR Australia, Vicinity Centres, Intract Australia, McMahon Services, and MSS Security. The College welcomes businesses interested in establishing scholarship programs. Sponsorship amounts are flexible, allowing sponsors to make either one-time payments or regular contributions.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled at SEDA College NT; Specific eligibility depends on individual sponsor requirements; Contact the College for details on available sponsored scholarships
special_conditions: Scholarships are sponsored by external organisations and conditions vary by sponsor. The College works with sponsors to establish mutually beneficial partnerships.

school_name: SEDA College NT - St Mary's Club campus
overview: SEDA College NT offers Indigenous Leaders Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. In 2025, five students received scholarships from multiple corporate sponsors dedicated to supporting Indigenous learners.
description: In 2025, five SEDA College NT students received an Indigenous Leaders Scholarship from corporate sponsors including DDR Australia, Vicinity Centres, Intract Australia, McMahon Services, and MSS Security. These scholarships provided students and their families with financial contributions towards their education and related support needs. The scholarships are designed to support Indigenous students in completing their senior secondary education and developing skills for successful careers. DDR Australia, a majority Aboriginal-owned contracting and project management company, is deeply committed to creating opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander employees. The exact financial value of each scholarship varies depending on sponsor contributions and individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Must be enrolled at SEDA College NT; Selection based on demonstrated leadership potential and commitment to education
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded through partnership between the College and corporate sponsors. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated leadership and commitment to their educational journey.

overview: SEDA College NT offers a Scholarship Program that celebrates achievement and contribution across key areas of college life. The program recognises students who demonstrate excellence and make significant contributions to the college community.

overview: SEDA College NT offers Fee Assistance (Bursaries) to support families experiencing genuine financial hardship. This program provides financial support to eligible families to help make education accessible.
description: SEDA College NT offers fee assistance in the form of bursaries to families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The College may offer assistance to families in genuine need who are current fee account holders. Due to limited funds being available, not all families who apply for fee assistance will be successful in receiving support. Families must complete a Fee Assistance Application Form and may be required to attend an interview to discuss their application. The specific amount of assistance available is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual circumstances and available funding.
eligibility_criteria: Current college fee account holders; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Genuine financial need required; Must complete Fee Assistance Application Form
special_conditions: Limited funds available; not all applicants will be successful. College Fee Account Holders may be asked to attend an interview to discuss their application. Application Form can be requested by emailing finance@sedacollege.nt.edu.au

overview: SEDA College NT partners with local businesses and organisations to offer Sponsored Scholarships. These scholarships create valuable connections between students and industry through corporate partnerships.

overview: St John's Catholic College offers Study NT Scholarships to international students seeking to study in Australia's Northern Territory. These scholarships, provided by the Northern Territory Government, reduce tuition costs for eligible international students enrolling at the College.
description: Study NT Scholarships are provided by the Northern Territory Government to promote international students to study in Australia's Northern Territory. The scholarships reduce the tuition for the student's first year or semester of study, valued at $5,000. International students must complete a Study NT Scholarship application form through the Study in Australia's Northern Territory Scholarship program. Full terms and conditions are available on the Study NT website at studynt.nt.gov.au. Applications must be submitted to the Study NT program directly, and eligibility is restricted to international students enrolling at St John's Catholic College.
eligibility_criteria: International students; First year or first semester of study; Must apply through Study NT program; Available to students from countries including Indonesia, Timor Leste, China, India, Thailand, Philippines, Ghana, Nepal, Bangladesh, Vietnam, Nigeria, Malaysia, Kenya, Brazil and Papua New Guinea
special_conditions: Scholarship applies to first year or first semester of study only. Administered through Study NT government program, not directly by the College. Full terms and conditions available on Study NT website.

overview: St John's Catholic College offers the Sr Anne Corbett Award to recognize and support students from Timor Leste background. This $2,000 annual scholarship is awarded to Year 10 or Year 11 students who demonstrate exceptional personal qualities aligned with Sr Anne's legacy.
description: The Sr Anne Corbett Award is a $2,000 scholarship provided annually as a contribution toward tuition for the following year. The scholarship is awarded to either a Year 10 or Year 11 student from a Timor Leste background. The student who is honoured must have demonstrated the qualities of Sr Anne Corbett: friendship, kindness, honesty and compassion. This award reflects the College's commitment to supporting students from culturally diverse backgrounds, particularly those with connections to Timor Leste, and recognizes exceptional character and values alignment with the College's Catholic mission.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 or Year 11 student; Must be from Timor Leste background; Must demonstrate qualities of friendship, kindness, honesty and compassion; Award contribution to tuition for following year
special_conditions: Award is provided annually as contribution to tuition for following year. Selection based on demonstration of personal qualities: friendship, kindness, honesty and compassion. Established through philanthropic donation to the College.
